risk_notes: Synthetic hook smoke test only; it validates local command classification, not every runtime tool payload shape.
setup: Run from CODEX_HOME after compact hook routing changes.
success_criteria: config.d/20-hooks.toml routes all lifecycle events to compact-codex-hook.ps1
config.d/20-hooks.toml covers Desktop tool namespaces that may carry risky payloads
The compact hook writes only its local ledger under .codex/state
UserPromptSubmit emits a compact scaffold reminder and candidate-claim calibration text
SessionStart emits a compact runtime cleanup reminder
PreToolUse allows ordinary inspected tool use and records the event
PreToolUse blocks direct credential-file read probes instead of unconditionally allowing them
PreToolUse blocks MCP credential-file read probes instead of only checking shell probes
PreToolUse allows non-file MCP getter tools that mention sensitive filenames without reading paths
PreToolUse blocks more/token-style direct secret reads without blocking safe reference searches
PreToolUse blocks interpreter payloads that directly target sensitive files
PreToolUse blocks nested multi_tool_use secret-read payloads before allowing the wrapper call
PreToolUse safe-reference exceptions do not allow secret-like search targets
PreToolUse blocks Select-String only when the explicit or positional path target is sensitive
PreToolUse blocks broad recursive destructive operations regardless of argument order
PreToolUse treats $HOME and $PWD as broad recursive destructive targets
PreToolUse blocks recursive deletes of current or parent relative roots, including common Windows aliases
PreToolUse blocks nested multi_tool_use broad destructive payloads before allowing the wrapper call
PostToolUse and Stop are record-only and do not claim completion authority
Retired hook config names are absent from active hook routing
Synthetic hook smoke tests use an isolated CODEX_HOME and restore the smoke ledger after probing
Windows hook commands use a hidden top-level PowerShell wrapper without cmd /c foreground windows
Retired archive, disabled-skill, and backup roots are validator failures after cleanup authorization
task: Verify the compact hook stays minimal, deterministic, and aligned with AGENTS.md instead of reintroducing legacy hook enforcement or archive-first cleanup.
